title: Day Tracker: Add summary stats panel (all-time, week, today hours & minutes)

requirements.text: In frontend/src/pages/DayTracker.tsx, add a prominent statistics summary panel at the top of the page (above the entry list) that displays four metrics computed from the user's day tracker entries stored in localStorage: (1) 'All Days Hours' — the total sum of all logged hours across every date ever recorded, (2) 'This Week Hours' — the sum of logged hours for entries whose date falls within the current calendar week (Monday to Sunday), (3) 'Today Hours' — the sum of logged hours for entries whose date matches today's ISO date, and (4) 'Today Minutes' — the equivalent of today's total hours expressed in minutes (e.g., 2.5 hours → 150 minutes). Display each metric in its own card with a clear label and a large numeric value. All calculations must react to changes in the entries data (i.e., update immediately when entries are added, edited, or deleted). Use date-fns or dayjs (already present in the project) for date comparisons and week boundary calculations.

quotes: on day track show all days hours, week hours, today hours , today minutes

acceptanceCriteria: A stats panel is visible on the Day Tracker page with four cards: 'All Days Hours', 'This Week Hours', 'Today Hours', and 'Today Minutes'.
'All Days Hours' shows the correct sum of all entry hours across all dates in the user's namespaced localStorage store.
'This Week Hours' shows the correct sum of hours for entries with a date falling in the current Mon–Sun week.
'Today Hours' shows the correct sum of hours for entries dated today.
'Today Minutes' shows today's total hours converted to total minutes (e.g., 1.5 hours = 90 minutes).
All four values update immediately (without page reload) when an entry is added, edited, completed, or deleted.
The stats panel is styled consistently with the existing teal/slate-green theme and is fully responsive.
If there are no entries, all four values display as 0.

constraints: Do not modify any immutable paths: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui.
Use the existing namespaced localStorage key (acadmind_day_entries_<userId>) already implemented in useQueries.ts for reading entry data.
Use date-fns or dayjs for all date/week boundary calculations — do not implement manual date arithmetic.
Do not alter the existing entry list, edit/delete functionality, daily summary section, or 7-day chart on the DayTracker page.
nonGoals: Modifying the entry data schema or localStorage key structure.
Changes to any other page or component outside DayTracker.tsx.
Adding charts or graphs for the new summary stats (plain metric cards are sufficient).
